title: MENA-PERC
text: *MENA-PERC** is a research project funded by an ERC Consolidator Grant (CoG 2021, Grant agreement ID 101044015) that studies the structure, behavior, and transformation of political elites across the Middle East and North Africa with a focus on Egypt, Tunisia, and Turkey. The project combines large-scale data collection with computational text analysis to examine how elite coalitions evolve, how power is distributed within regimes, and how these dynamics shape processes of regime stability, crisis, and authoritarian transformation.

title: MENA-PERC
text: *MENA-PERC** is a research project funded by an ERC Consolidator Grant (CoG 2021, Grant agreement ID 101044015) that studies the structure, behavior, and transformation of political elites across the Middle East and North Africa with a focus on Egypt, Tunisia, and Turkey. The project combines large-scale data collection with computational text analysis to examine how elite coalitions evolve, how power is distributed within regimes, and how these dynamics shape processes of regime stability, crisis, and authoritarian transformation.

title: MENA-PERC
text:
🔍 About
MENA-PERC focuses on the systematic analysis of political elites using original datasets on appointments, careers, and legislative behavior. A central goal of the project is to understand how intra-elite relations—such as cooperation, competition, and exclusion—affect political outcomes, including democratization, authoritarian consolidation, and conflict.
The project integrates: - Large-scale data on political appointments and careers
- Legislative speech corpora and text-as-data approaches
- Theoretical frameworks on elite power-sharing and regime dynamics

Empirical analyses of elite behavior during regime crises
